summary.brief: Peng v. Meeks. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: This medical malpractice case stems from the withdrawal of life-sustaining treatment that resulted in the death of Lillian Birt.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: While Ms. Birt was in the intensive care unit (ICU), her children decided to discontinue life support based on their understanding of conversations they had with their mother’s treating doctors about her condition.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In fact, their mother’s condition was not terminal; there was a high likelihood that she would have recovered if treatment had been continued.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury ultimately found that the doctors acted negligently in connection with the withdrawal of care and awarded damages to the heirs on the wrongful death claim and to the estate on the survival claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We hold that the district court correctly instructed the jury that Ms. Meeks had the burden of proving the standard of care to establish medical malpractice.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: But we hold that the court erred in denying the doctors’ motion for judgment as a matter of law on the survival claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: After experiencing complications from a surgery performed by Dr. Christina Richards, Lillian Birt was diagnosed with pneumonia and sepsis and was placed on life support.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A few days before Ms. Birt’s death, the hospital staff conducted 'weaning trials' to determine whether Ms. Birt could be taken off the ventilator.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Meeks testified that during the weaning trials, the medical staff 'had to take her [mother] off what was keeping her asleep or not in pain.'
evidence_index.speaker: Ms. Meeks

evidence_index.text: Dr. Peng testified that she 'had a very good chance to return to another care facility if we [had kept] going.'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Peng

evidence_index.text: Dr. Peng gave Ms. Meeks a form to sign indicating that Ms. Birt’s condition was 'terminal' and that '[t]o continue providing life-saving procedures would only serve to unnaturally prolong the moment of death.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We affirm in part and reverse in part.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Lillian Birt experienced complications from a surgery performed by Dr. Christina Richards, leading to pneumonia and sepsis. She was placed on life support and later admitted to the ICU.

reveal.result_summary: Patient admitted to ICU with respiratory failure and septic shock

requestables.name: Family Meeting
requestables.description: Discussion with family regarding patient's prognosis and treatment options

reveal.result_summary: Family informed of terminal condition, consent obtained for withdrawal of care
reveal.clinical_significance: Informed consent for withdrawal of life support
reveal.decision_impact: Led to withdrawal of life-sustaining treatment

timeline_phases.name: ICU Admission
timeline_phases.description: Patient admitted to ICU with complications from surgery

timeline_phases.name: Decision to Withdraw Care
timeline_phases.description: Family informed of prognosis and consent obtained for withdrawal of life support

key_events.event: Family consented to withdrawal of life support

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ICU with life support
decision_points.prompt: What information should be provided to the family regarding the patient's prognosis?

options.description: Inform family of high likelihood of recovery and potential for meaningful recovery
options.clinical_reasoning: Accurate prognosis should be communicated to ensure informed decision-making

options.description: Inform family that patient's condition is terminal and recommend withdrawal of care
options.clinical_reasoning: Misleading information led to premature withdrawal of care

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant informed family of terminal condition and obtained consent for withdrawal
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant stated it was to respect family's wishes

expected_action_court.description: Family should have been informed of the high likelihood of recovery
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established that accurate prognosis communication is standard

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Family should have been informed of recovery potential
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient died following withdrawal of life-sustaining treatment

key_findings: Breach of duty in withdrawing life-sustaining treatment
Causation established - withdrawal led to patient's death

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Incorrect assessment of patient's condition as terminal
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Continued treatment would have allowed recovery

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to ICU with complications from surgery

complications.complication: Death following withdrawal of life support

allegations.allegation: Failure to inform family of recovery potential

court_findings.legal_significance: Breach of standard of care in assessing patient's condition
